source: TC-Budgets V2 Meeting Actions AND Data (4 Nov 2025)

stories.title: Support item-based rate cards (quantity/price model)
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to add budget items like falls alarms, nutritional powder, and equipment using quantity + price model (not hours + rate), so that I can allocate non-hourly services correctly.
stories.impact: 🔴 I can't do my job without this
acceptance_criteria: Budget item creation supports 'Item' service type with quantity/price fields
Rate card section exists for item-based services separate from hourly services
Can specify unit price and quantity
Calculations correctly multiply quantity × unit price
UI clearly distinguishes item-based vs hourly service planning
stories.feedback_source: Budget items like falls alarm, nutritional powder, items etc. needs its own rate card section where its quantity and price rather than only hours and rate.

stories.title: Expand frequency options for maintenance services
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need frequency options beyond weekly/fortnightly/monthly (specifically: 3-weekly, 6-weekly, 8-weekly, 13-weekly, biannual, annual), so that I can accurately plan maintenance services that don't fit standard patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Frequency dropdown includes: weekly, fortnightly, 3-weekly, 4-weekly (monthly), 6-weekly, 8-weekly, 13-weekly, biannual, annual
Frequency options available for all applicable service types
Form validates correctly with all frequency options
Calculations (4-weekly totals) work correctly with all frequencies
stories.feedback_source: Frequency on services like home maintenance need more frequencies than weekly, f/n, mth, as required. (it needs 3, 6, 8, 13 weekly, biannual, and annual)

stories.title: Add 'requires falls alarm' option to needs section
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need a 'requires falls alarm' option in the needs dropdown (not just available when already accessing it), so that I can record fall intervention needs upfront.
acceptance_criteria: 'Requires falls alarm' appears as a selectable option in the needs dropdown
Works independently (not dependent on existing falls alarm access)
Can be toggled on/off during budget creation
Syncs to care plan correctly
stories.feedback_source: No option for requires falls alarm, only if they are already accessing it (drop down menu needs section)

stories.title: Funding stream allocation priority logic
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need budgets to allocate to quarterly funding first, then unspent funds (when both are selected), so that funding allocation follows the correct priority and doesn't create glitches requiring re-selection.
stories.impact: 🔴 I can't do my job without this
acceptance_criteria: When both quarterly and unspent funds are selected, quarterly funds allocated first
Unspent funds only allocated after quarterly budget exhausted
No need to unselect/reselect unspent to fix allocation order
Allocation order visible and confirmed before submit
Tested with hybrid funding scenarios
stories.feedback_source: Budgets using unspent funds instead of using up quarterly budget first when both funding streams selected, is this a glitch? If I unselect unspent, save, reselect unspent it fixes it

stories.title: Quarterly vs total planned view toggle
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to toggle between 'total planned' and 'planned this quarter' for unspent funds display, so that I can focus on quarterly budget planning without seeing multi-quarter totals.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle switch visible on budget page
'Total planned' shows cumulative across all quarters
'Planned this quarter' shows only current quarter allocation
Toggle state persists for user session
Correctly updates when changing date range
stories.feedback_source: Has there been any update on Unspent Funds only showing from the quarter we are planning in? Would it at least be possible to add a toggle to switch from 'total planned' to 'planned this quarter'?

stories.title: Add 'Every 3 weeks' frequency option
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need a 'every 3 weeks' frequency option, so that I can accommodate coordinator requests for non-standard frequencies.
acceptance_criteria: 'Every 3 weeks' appears in frequency dropdown
4-weekly total calculated correctly
Saves and persists correctly
stories.feedback_source: Can we add every three week in the options, as i have had a few client ask for this option.

stories.title: Display total coordination fee for period
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need a field showing total coordination fee for the period, so that I can see at a glance how much coordination cost is budgeted.
acceptance_criteria: New field 'Total Coordination Fee' displays on budget page
Automatically calculates sum of all coordination fees for the period
Updates when coordination services are added/removed
Formatted as currency
stories.feedback_source: Can you add a field in the Portal which shows the 'Total Coordination fee' for the period.

stories.title: Suppress supplementary budget fields by default
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need supplementary/extra budget fields to appear on care plan by default (not require clicking dropdown), so that I can see all planned services without extra navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Supplements section visible on initial load (not collapsed)
Can still collapse if desired
Shows summary of supplements in collapsed state
Clearly labeled as 'Supplements' or 'Additional Services'
stories.feedback_source: Supplements don't show on service plan page

stories.title: Clarify funding stream eligibility per service
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need UI clarity about which funding streams each service CAN and CANNOT be assigned to, so that I understand eligibility rules upfront.
acceptance_criteria: Service type shows allowed funding stream(s) or funding stream restrictions
Tooltips or help text explain why certain streams aren't available
Error message if trying to assign to ineligible stream is explanatory
Tested with all service types
stories.feedback_source: UI could be more explanatory about which funding stream different services CAN and CANNOT be assigned to

stories.title: Fix supplement visibility on service plan
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need supplements to show on the service plan page, so that I can verify all allocated services before submission.
acceptance_criteria: Supplements section appears on service plan page
All selected supplements listed with quantities/details
Clearly distinguished from primary services
Visible in both draft and submitted states
stories.feedback_source: Supplements don't show on service plan page - Could you please advise where I can check if the client has a supplement included under the new budget?

stories.title: Batch save budget amendments
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to save all budget amendments in one action (not individual saves), so that I can edit multiple services efficiently without waiting for each to load.
acceptance_criteria: Single 'Save All' button at top-right or bottom of form (like 'Submit')
All pending changes saved in one request
Loading occurs once after all changes submitted
Success confirmation shows all changes saved
Validation occurs for all fields before save
stories.feedback_source: Having to save each budget amendments individually and having to wait for them to load one after another can be a bit counterproductive... Can we have one save button... would save a lot more time.

stories.title: Rename duplicate action to 'Edit'
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need the action to edit a submitted plan called 'Edit' (not 'Duplicate'), so that the UI is intuitive for making changes.
acceptance_criteria: Button/menu item labeled 'Edit' instead of 'Duplicate'
Maintains same functionality (creates new version for editing)
Help text clarifies that editing submitted plans creates a new revision
stories.feedback_source: UI is not intuitive to knowing that you need to duplicate a plan to make any edits to a submitted plan. Edit is the expected terminology

stories.title: Minimalist SAH budget screen design
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need the SAH budget screen to load faster with a minimalist design, so that switching between old and new budget screens isn't slow.
acceptance_criteria: Page load time < 2 seconds for typical budget (benchmark: measure baseline first)
Minimized visual clutter (remove non-essential elements initially visible)
Summary view on hover; details expand on click (not all visible by default)
Performance tested on standard laptop hardware
stories.feedback_source: Is there a way that we can make the new SAH budget screen more 'minimalist'; I would think this could make the transitions/loading up of the new budget quicker

stories.title: Summary view similar to old budget interface
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need a summary view (like the old budget) shown on hover, with detailed breakdown only on dropdown click, so that I can quickly scan budgets without opening full details.
acceptance_criteria: Hovering over budget line shows summary (Frequency, Units, Rates, 4-weekly Total)
Click dropdown to expand full details (planned/projected spend)
Faster rendering with lazy-loaded detail sections
Works on both desktop and laptop
stories.feedback_source: Maybe switching it up and present the summary of the budget (when we hover over the budget line) and only show the entire budget details inc. planned/projected to spend when we click the drop down bar.

stories.title: Budget change visibility/comparison
stories.description: As a care coordinator receiving a budget change, I need to see what changed (not just the new version), so that I can approve or reject changes confidently.
acceptance_criteria: When CO submits budget change, 'View Changes' or 'Compare' option available
Shows side-by-side diff of old vs new (or highlights changed fields)
Clearly indicates which line items changed
Approve/Reject buttons appear after reviewing changes
stories.feedback_source: When CO sent a budget change, I don't see what has been changed and not sure what to approve... as before, I could easily see the change request and reject or approve straight away

stories.title: Fix form validation inconsistency (required field marking)
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need form validation to be consistent (fields with * required, without * optional), so that I don't save incomplete forms and get errors.
acceptance_criteria: All required fields marked with *
All optional fields NOT marked with *
Server-side validation rejects saves with missing required fields
Error message specifies which required fields are missing
Tested across all budget forms
stories.feedback_source: Fields with * are able to be saved without being filled, then details with no * on the text box is required to be filled (description in needs)

stories.title: Auto-expand needs field by default
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need the needs field to appear by default with a checkbox to uncheck if not needed (not hidden by default), so that I don't miss recording important care needs.
acceptance_criteria: Needs section visible on initial page load
Checkbox 'Include in care plan' checked by default
Can uncheck to exclude from care plan
Helps prevent missed data entry
stories.feedback_source: It would be good if it automatically had the needs entry show up on care plan and have a tick box if you don't want it, rather than vice versa.

stories.title: Display screen unblurred on laptop
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need the budget screen to render clearly on laptop without blur, so that I can read and edit budgets properly.
acceptance_criteria: No blur/rendering issues on standard laptop resolutions (1920x1080, 1366x768)
Text clarity verified at 100% and 125% zoom
Tested on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
Performance acceptable on older laptops
stories.feedback_source: Coordinators raising that screen is blurred on laptop

stories.title: Outlook email tagging add-in
stories.description: As a care team member, I need an Outlook add-in that intercepts send and prompts me to tag emails before sending, so that all budget-related emails are categorized correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Event-based Outlook add-in listens for Send button
Custom popup appears with tag dropdown
Cannot send email until tag selected
Tags stored in email metadata
Works with Outlook desktop and web versions
stories.technical_approach: 1. Intercept Send event via Office.js; 2. Open custom task pane popup; 3. Display dropdown with available tagging options; 4. Block send until selection made; 5. Store tag in email custom properties
stories.feedback_source: Create an event-based add in for Outlook: 1. Run a code when the user clicks send 2. open a custom pop-up for tagging 3. Block the send until the tagging is complete

stories.title: Service plan sent email template
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need configurable email templates for service plan notifications, so that clients receive consistent, branded communication about their budgets.
acceptance_criteria: Template variables: [ClientName], [ServicePlanDate], [CoordinatorName], [ContactInfo]
Admin can customize template text and formatting
Draft and live versions available
Tested with bulk send tool
stories.feedback_source: Provide service plan sent email template to Tim (Action: Romy to create post-Nov 1 template)

stories.title: Include care coordinator name in 'Service Plans' inbox
stories.description: As a clinical team member, I need to see the care coordinator name in the Service Plans inbox, so that I know who to contact about budget changes.
acceptance_criteria: Care coordinator name visible in inbox list
Name appears in budget detail view
Clickable to open coordinator contact info
stories.feedback_source: Can we include the CR name in the 'Service Plans' Inbox?

stories.title: Change service plan PDF language to 'planned with you'
stories.description: As a client receiving a service plan PDF, I need the language to reflect collaborative planning ('planned with you') instead of paternalistic ('planned for you'), so that I feel heard in the planning process.
acceptance_criteria: PDF template updated: 'A list of services planned for you' → 'A list of services planned with you'
Language updated consistently across all service plan sections
Tested in generated PDF output
stories.feedback_source: Can we change this to 'planned with you' to reflect the collaborative process and requirements

stories.title: Mandatory action plan for all risks
stories.description: As a clinical coordinator, I need all risks to have mandatory action plan sections (not just yes/no), so that I can document how we're addressing identified risks.
stories.impact: 🔴 I can't do my job without this
acceptance_criteria: All risk types have mandatory action plan field
Cannot save risk without populated action plan
Field supports multi-line text entry
Visible in care plan view and print
stories.feedback_source: Not all risks have a action plan section that is mandatory, when it should be... We always used to have details and action plans in place. Now we don't.

stories.title: Add EPOA and AHD recording fields
stories.description: As a clinical coordinator, I need clear fields for recording EPOA and AHD documents, so that I can track legal authority without searching through supplemental data.
stories.impact: 🔴 I can't do my job without this
acceptance_criteria: Dedicated EPOA field in care plan
Dedicated AHD field in care plan
Can upload or reference documents
Visible in quick reference on overview page
stories.feedback_source: EPOA and AHD does not have a clear spot to be recorded. This is sometimes placed under the overview in the portal care plan and is exclusively recorded using the current onboarding questionnaire

stories.title: Add Country of Birth field
stories.description: As an operations team member managing government reporting, I need a 'Country of Birth' field separate from 'Nationality', so that we can satisfy end-of-life questionnaire requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Country of Birth field appears in client demographics
Separate from Nationality field
Searchable dropdown of countries
Required for government reporting workflows
stories.feedback_source: We also need to record Country of Birth for clients in addition to Nationality. This was something that we could not find during a EOL questionnaire from the government.

stories.title: Add pie charts to collapsed funding panel
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to see pie chart visualizations in the collapsed funding panel state, so that I can quickly see funding stream distribution at a glance without expanding the full panel.
acceptance_criteria: Collapsed funding panel displays mini pie charts for each active funding stream
Pie charts visually represent plan/spent/projected proportions
Clicking on a pie or the panel expands to full funding detail view
Pie charts update in real-time as services are added/modified
Works for 2-5 concurrent funding streams without visual clutter
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Decision to add pies to collapsed state for quick visual funding stream reference

stories.title: Display remaining balance for each funding stream in left sidebar
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to see the remaining cash balance for each funding stream in the left sidebar (visible across all package pages), so that I always know what funds are available without navigating to the budget page.
stories.impact: 🔴 I can't do my job without this
acceptance_criteria: Left sidebar displays remaining balance for each active funding stream
Shows cash-based remaining (not projected) - calculated as: Total Funding - Approved Invoice Spend
Visible on all package context pages (Claims, Budget, Documents, etc.)
Updates when invoices are approved
Color-coded per funding stream (e.g., ON = blue, HC = green)
Time-window aware - shows funding streams only when active (e.g., restorative care for 6 weeks, ATHM for 12 months)
Clickable to navigate to budget/funding detail page
Displays as small, minimalist UI cards/badges to handle 2-5 funding streams without clutter
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Decision to show very small representation of remaining amount for all funding streams in left-bar, cash first

stories.title: Add funding stream tooltip with supplement details
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to see supplement dollar amounts in tooltips when hovering over funding stream indicators, so that I can understand what's contributing to the total funding amount.
acceptance_criteria: Hovering over funding stream pie/card shows tooltip with breakdown
Tooltip displays: Oxygen supplement ($X), Enteral feeding supplement ($X), Dementia supplement ($X), etc.
Shows date ranges for each supplement
Shows MSO percentage and dollar value
Tooltip appears on both collapsed and expanded funding views
Clear, readable formatting for 3-5 supplements
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Decision to add $amount of each supplement inside the tooltip

stories.title: Display funding stream expiry date and time-to-expiry warning
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to see when each funding stream will expire (based on planned spend), so that I can proactively plan for package level reviews or contribution changes.
acceptance_criteria: Funding stream detail shows calculated expiry date based on current plan
Warning icon appears when funding will run out before end of current planning period
Example message: 'In 45 days you will be out of funds' or 'Expected to expire: March 15, 2026'
Warning appears for both spend-based expiry (ON, HC) and time-based expiry (ATHM 12 months)
Does not plan services beyond the expiry date for that funding stream
Warning visible on funding panel and/or as icon next to funding stream name
ATHM expiry prominently displayed (12-month limit from activation)
Time-window funding streams (restorative care, ATHM) clearly communicate their limited availability
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Decision to show date that funding expires and warning 'in 45 days you will be out of funds'

stories.title: Remove overall funding rollup from right panel
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need to see individual funding stream balances (not a combined total), so that I don't misunderstand what funds are available when they have different time constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Remove gray 'Total Available' rollup box from funding panel
Display only individual funding streams (ON, HC, AT-HM, RC, VC, etc.)
Each funding stream shows its own plan/spent/projected/remaining
No aggregated total that could mislead users about constrained funds (e.g., mixing quarterly ON with 6-week restorative care)
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Decision: no rollup, show individual funding streams only

stories.title: Display participant contributions based on fortnightly cap
stories.description: As a care coordinator working with grandfathered clients, I need contributions to be calculated using the fortnightly cap (not monthly percentage), so that clients see accurate expected contributions and don't feel worse off.
acceptance_criteria: Contribution calculations use fortnightly cap for grandfathered clients
Hybrid and new clients continue using monthly percentage method
Contribution display shows accurate expected amounts based on service plan
Calculation logic documented in ticket for cap implementation
Tested with grandfathered client scenarios to ensure accuracy
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Decision to show contributions based on fortnightly cap, create ticket for cap logic

stories.title: Rename 'Coord Loading' to 'Coord Overheads'
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need the coordination cost label to say 'Coord Overheads' (not 'Coord Loading'), so that the terminology is clearer and more accurate.
acceptance_criteria: All instances of 'Coord Loading' changed to 'Coord Overheads'
Updated in budget UI, service plan, funding breakdown, and any tooltips
Tested across all budget and funding views
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Decision to change Coord Loading to Coord Overheads

stories.title: Maintain 'Care to Activate' utilization modal
stories.description: As a care coordinator, I need access to the 'Care to Activate' utilization modal, so that I can see detailed utilization metrics when needed.
acceptance_criteria: Utilization link appears under funding stream section (e.g., under 'Home Support Ongoing')
Clicking link opens utilization modal
Modal displays 'Care to Activate' and other utilization metrics
Modal content updated to match new funding stream UI patterns
Does not replace inline funding stream displays
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Confirmed utilization modal exists, needs content rework in future

stories.title: Investigate OT prescription service funding stream assignment
stories.description: As a technical lead, I need to investigate how OT prescription services can be reliably identified and charged to the ATHM funding stream (as opposed to OT services charged to ongoing funding), so that invoices are correctly allocated.
acceptance_criteria: Research completed on distinguishing 'Clinical Prescription' (ATHM-eligible) from 'Occupational Therapy' (ON-eligible)
Document current invoice matching logic and gaps
Propose solution for reliable funding stream assignment (e.g., service ID, prescription module flag, invoice notes matching)
Consider edge cases: invoices submitted through prescriptions module, generic 'OT' invoices
Findings documented in discovery ticket
Recommendation provided for budget UI (e.g., separate 'Clinical Prescription' service type vs OT)
stories.feedback_source: BUR Meeting Transcript 9/12/25 - Discussion about ensuring OT prescription services can be reliably ID'd and charged to ATHM funding stream vs ON
